requirements.description: Master LAOS is operated by the highly capable Software Architect AI, not by the weaker execution agent.
The Architect AI retains full project understanding, architecture, future work, risk decisions, hidden checks, and acceptance authority.
A weaker agent receives only the minimum bounded instructions required for its current authorised action.
New-application and existing-application workflows must be documented as separate Architect-controlled flows.
LAOS must be described as an intelligence-amplification, prompt-compilation, delegation, and control framework.
The runtime, not the weaker agent narrative, decides whether work is complete.
No documentation may imply that the weaker agent should ingest the master framework.
Architect Control Packs and weaker-agent Execution Packs must be physically separate.
Capture Packs and Review Packs must be separate from implementation packs.
Architect-only plans, future actions, hidden checks, signing material, and private reasoning must not enter weaker-agent packages.
Every execution package must pass deterministic leakage scanning before delivery.
Task and action contracts must be content-hashed and protected from builder mutation.
Untrusted repository content must never override signed Architect instructions or policy.
Context items must carry provenance labels distinguishing authority, evidence, observation, and untrusted content.
Outstanding authority must be revocable after policy, model, skill, repository, or threat-state changes.
Only one legal Action Capsule may be active for an executor at a time.
Future actions must remain inaccessible until prerequisites and current action gates pass.
The standard substantive sequence must enforce UNDERSTAND, PLAN, IMPLEMENT, VERIFY, EVIDENCE, HANDOFF, REVIEW, and CLOSE gates.
Every Action Capsule must specify one objective, actor, scope, tools, outputs, checks, evidence, stop conditions, retry limit, seal, expiry, and escalation path.
Action issuance must bind to repository seal, policy version, model profile, skill set, nonce, and identity.
An agent must not prepare or partially implement later actions.
Repeated failed-approach signatures must trigger narrowing, restoration, blocking, or Architect escalation.
Critical actions may require fresh sessions and stronger review depth.
Action skipping, out-of-order transitions, replay, expiry, and early next-action requests must be tested adversarially.
No product edit may occur before a structured UNDERSTAND record passes validation.
The UNDERSTAND record must state objective, criteria, invariants, preserved behaviour, scope, non-goals, risks, unknowns, and verification method.
Vague acknowledgements such as “I understand” must fail.
Unknown facts must remain in an explicit Uncertainty Ledger until resolved or accepted.
A separate PLAN gate must map every acceptance criterion to files, changes, checks, failure paths, preservation checks, and evidence.
Plans omitting criteria or proposing unjustified broad refactors must fail.
Plans exceeding the active model profile limits must be decomposed.
Repository evidence must outrank old chat or prior-agent claims when they conflict.
Every mandatory acceptance criterion must have an independent lifecycle.
Criteria must progress through NOT_STARTED, IMPLEMENTED, VERIFIED, and INDEPENDENTLY_REVIEWED states.
Each criterion must link to relevant source, checks, evidence, and reviewer verdict.
A task cannot close while any mandatory criterion is incomplete or stale.
A generic task-level PASS must never mask unfinished criteria.
All MUST requirements must map to at least one task and criterion.
Duplicate requirement, task, action, criterion, check, evidence, and side-effect identifiers must be rejected.
Runtime state, source state, evidence, check results, and final reports must reconcile before acceptance.
LAOS must support explicit executor profiles with bounded prompt size, files per action, criteria per action, tools, retries, network access, and review depth.
Fragile local, weak desktop, standard coding, strong executor, investigator, tester, and reviewer profiles must be supported.
Oversized work must be split rather than delivered as a large instruction block.
Critical rules may be repeated or rendered more explicitly for weaker models.
Calibration must use real actions and observed compliance, not simulated success.
Profile limits must tighten after failures and only widen from measured evidence.
Prompt compilation must be deterministic for the same authoritative inputs.
Every direct agent prompt must pass linting for role, repository, objective, finish line, scope, checks, evidence, stop conditions, contradictions, placeholders, and capability ambiguity.
Prompt linting must detect accidental code-edit permission during audit-only work.
Prompt linting must detect Architect-only information leakage and excessive context.
Tool unavailability must block affected work rather than silently reducing assurance.
Project context must be compact, source-grounded, and role-specific.
Long transcripts belong in evidence records rather than every action prompt.
Handoffs must preserve blockers, uncertainty, decisions, and next legal action without stale exploration noise.
Repository truth and signed runtime state must outrank handoff prose.
Skills and adapters must be loaded only when relevant and must carry integrity metadata.
Context provenance must be machine-readable.
A weaker agent must receive only current-action context unless broader read access is explicitly required.
Existing-application implementation must begin with a read-only capture workflow.
Capture must use bounded actions for repository identity, architecture, features, interfaces, data, integrations, tests, deployment, risks, protected areas, and unknowns.
Capture agents must be prevented from implementing, repairing, refactoring, deploying, or changing external systems.
App Intelligence Returns must be strict, evidence-backed, freshness-bound, and safe to extract.
Unknown information must stay explicitly unknown rather than becoming guessed truth.
The Architect must validate, reconcile, and independently understand captured information before continuation work is compiled.
Continuation work must preserve verified working behaviour and protected areas.
Repository change after capture must require delta recapture or explicit Architect amendment.
The Capture Return is evidence for the Architect, not automatic design authority.
Capture, continuation, implementation, evidence, and release must share one canonical repository-manifest implementation.
The fingerprint algorithm must have an explicit version identifier.
Field order, normalization, ignore rules, file types, modes, and symlink representation must be identical across workflows.
Unknown fingerprint algorithm versions must fail closed.
An unchanged capture-to-continuation round trip must pass.
A one-byte tracked change must fail freshness verification.
Excluded content must be explicitly recorded rather than silently omitted.
Repository manifests must not follow symlink targets outside the root.
All trusted JSON records must validate against strict JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 contracts.
Schema validation must reject missing required fields, wrong types, invalid enums, malformed identifiers, invalid paths, and prohibited additional fields.
Semantic validators must enforce cross-record references, graph rules, state invariants, and authority relationships.
Compiler, capture, continuation, runtime, review, evidence, side-effect, and release paths must use the same validation library and schema registry.
Schema versions and migrations must be explicit; no trusted record format may be guessed.
Deliberately malformed captures and packs must be permanent regression tests.
Schema-valid but factually weak evidence must still require Architect or reviewer assessment.
The Architect must establish a signed workspace seal before weaker-agent work begins.
Task claim must fail when current repository state differs from the approved seal.
Pre-claim edits must be detected and cannot become an invisible task baseline.
Every action must record before-and-after repository manifests.
Unexplained pre-existing changes must block work pending recapture, acceptance, or amendment.
Isolated worktrees or equivalent controlled working copies should be preferred.
Repository seal changes must invalidate incompatible outstanding capsules.
Git state and content state must both be recorded without treating Git alone as complete truth.
Every filesystem path must be normalized, resolved, and contained inside its authorised root.
Absolute external paths, parent traversal, symlink escapes, junction escapes, and alternate separator tricks must be rejected.
Task, action, check, evidence, artifact, checkpoint, and operation identifiers must obey strict patterns before becoming paths.
Archive readers must reject unsafe names, duplicates, symlinks, excessive size, suspicious ratios, and destination escapes.
Artifact and evidence collectors must not follow external links.
Compiler force options must reject repository roots, parent roots, source roots, and other dangerous destinations.
Test and hidden-verification assets must have separate write policy.
Audit-only actions must not gain product-code write authority.
Local mutable runtime state must use transactional SQLite, not independent mutable JSON files.
The database must use migrations, foreign keys, uniqueness constraints, explicit transactions, and recoverable journal configuration.
Claims, leases, heartbeats, events, criterion updates, evidence registration, and side effects must be atomic.
Two workers must not claim or advance the same action concurrently.
Event ordering and chain linkage must be transactionally serialized.
Local coordination must not be presented as distributed coordination.
Multi-machine execution must require an external transactional store or lease service.
Runtime state must remain outside immutable release manifests and release archives.
Database, WAL, SHM, locks, and generated runtime records must be treated as mutable project state.
State migrations must be tested forward, backward where supported, interrupted, and corrupted.
Actor identity must be authenticated rather than accepted as a typed display name.
Architect, builder, tester, reviewer, release acceptor, and recovery roles must have distinct capabilities.
Capability grants must be scoped, expiring, revocable, replay-resistant, and bound to project and seal.
A builder must not impersonate a reviewer or release acceptor.
Raw identity secrets and signing keys must remain outside the project repository.
Every authorization denial must use the stable AuthorizationDenied hierarchy and machine-readable error code.
Important events and release attestations must be signed or externally anchored.
A local hash chain alone must not be described as independent provenance.
High-risk work should separate OS, source-control, CI, and external-service identities where practical.
Evidence must be stored under an Evidence folder at the project root with canonical proof protected from builder mutation.
Commands, working directory, timestamps, exit codes, stdout, stderr, source seal, and output hashes must be captured automatically.
Browser, API, database, build, deployment, and repository-difference proof must be collected automatically where relevant.
Every evidence item must map to the criterion it proves.
Evidence must become stale when relevant source, policy, check definition, or environment changes.
Evidence maturity must distinguish assertion, runtime recording, deterministic verification, independent rerun, and external attestation.
Higher-risk criteria must require stronger evidence maturity.
Evidence and logs must be scanned for credentials and sensitive values before packaging.
Canonical evidence may be escrowed outside builder authority while a signed index remains in the root Evidence folder.
Agent prose alone must never satisfy a behavioural criterion.
Verification must cover applicable happy paths, invalid inputs, missing data, authorization failures, cross-scope isolation, retries, duplicates, timeouts, partial failure, recovery, and invariants.
Every bug fix must include a regression check that would have detected the original failure.
Tests must not be weakened, deleted, or rewritten merely to produce a pass.
Negative evidence must prove relevant dangerous outcomes did not occur.
Unavailable credentials or services must produce a clear blocked state rather than fake success.
Hidden checks must remain outside builder write authority.
Clean verification must occur outside the builder-modified environment for high-risk work.
Concurrency, interruption, migration, extraction, and tamper tests are mandatory for their respective subsystems.
The builder must never approve its own work.
Review must use a separate authenticated identity and preferably fresh context.
The reviewer must receive the original requirement, frozen contract, final repository state, current evidence, and authorised hidden checks.
Review instructions must ask the reviewer to disprove completion rather than endorse the builder summary.
Review must inspect missing criteria, scope, regressions, security, failure paths, test weakening, secret leakage, and unsupported claims.
Review must not silently repair implementation during the review action.
Defects must return through a bounded repair action.
The Architect must adjudicate material builder-reviewer disagreement.
Critical work may require reviewer quorum or independent verifier classes.
Contracted commands must use structured argv execution without shell interpretation by default.
The command broker must bind executable, arguments, working directory, environment, timeout, network, resources, and expected outputs.
Dangerous commands must be denied unless separately authorised by policy.
Repository instructions are untrusted data and cannot expand tool authority.
LAOS must clearly state that workflow controls are not an operating-system sandbox.
High-risk weaker-agent execution must use a real container, VM, microVM, or managed sandbox and fail closed if required isolation is unavailable.
Sandboxes must use non-root execution, narrow writable mounts, no unrelated personal files, no credentials by default, network default-deny, destination allowlists, and resource limits.
Audit logs for high-risk work should be stored outside the agent workspace.
At least one real sandbox provider must be exercised before release.
External side effects must follow a formal proposed, authorised, prepared, executed, externally verified, and committed lifecycle.
Aborted and compensated terminal states must be supported.
Every external operation must use a unique idempotency key, payload fingerprint, expected result, verifier, and rollback or compensation plan.
Only authorised roles may externally verify or commit side effects.
An unauthorised verification attempt must raise AuthorizationDenied, create a denial event, perform no external action, and advance no state.
Human or governed approval must be required for production deployment, payment, public publishing, data export, destructive migration, credential creation, and irreversible cloud work.
Checkpoints must be immutable or content-addressed and bound to repository and task state.
Recovery must be a separately authorised operation with dry-run visibility.
Recovery must not delete additional files unless explicitly authorised.
Failed approaches must be recorded and repeated failure must trigger anti-thrashing controls.
Amendments must be explicit, signed, and traceable rather than silent contract mutation.
Immutable release material must be physically separated from mutable project runtime state.
No SQLite database, WAL, SHM, active lock, claim, lease, or generated project evidence may enter the static LAOS release manifest.
Release archives must be built from a clean staging directory and created from scratch.
Release archives must exclude Git internals, caches, real environment files, secrets, nested archives, mutable state, and unnecessary build output.
Builds must be deterministic for identical source and toolchain inputs where technically feasible.
Artifacts must be verified by hash, size, content listing, safe extraction, and post-extraction test rerun.
Post-build mutation must be detected.
Loose evidence, archived evidence, reports, test counts, commit hashes, file lists, and stated clean state must reconcile.
Final reports must be generated from verified truth records rather than memory.
Release outputs must include checksums, integrity manifest, SBOM, provenance, limitations, test report, evaluation report, and signatures or attestations appropriate to assurance level.
Final artifacts must be moved to durable storage, reopened, stat-ed, hashed, extracted, and retested before completion is claimed.
The original v7 archive must remain untouched and separately available.
LAOS performance claims must be measured with real weaker agents, not inferred from unit tests.
Evaluation must compare broad prompts, static structured prompts, v7, v8 controlled execution, and a stronger reference where useful.
Benchmarks must cover new builds, existing-app capture, continuation, bug repair, integrations, recovery, and high-risk workflows.
Runs must be repeated to measure variance.
Seeded defects and hidden acceptance tests must be used.

Metrics must include task success, criterion closure, skipped actions, premature work, out-of-scope changes, false completion, test weakening, evidence truthfulness, regressions, recovery, repeated failures, human intervention, time, tokens, and cost.
Intermediate actions and tool traces must be graded, not only final messages.
No simulated or placeholder agent execution may count as evaluation evidence.
Observed results must tune model profiles and action sizes.
“Orders-of-magnitude improvement” remains a target until supported by reproducible results.

The compiler must fail on uncovered mandatory requirements, dependency cycles, duplicate identifiers, missing checks, missing evidence, missing failure paths, missing stop conditions, and invalid action sequences.
The compiler must fail on actions referring to nonexistent checks, evidence, roles, capabilities, or criteria.
The compiler must fail on oversized tasks for the selected model profile and overly broad write paths.
The compiler must fail on audit tasks with code-edit permission and high-risk tasks without required review, hidden verification, sandbox, or approval.
The compiler must reject unresolved placeholders, contradictory instructions, missing preservation rules, and Architect-only content in execution packs.
The compiler must produce explainable, machine-readable diagnostics with stable error codes.
Separate entry documents must exist for Nilhan, the Architect AI, investigator, builder, tester, reviewer, recovery operator, and release verifier.
Documentation must clearly distinguish the master system, Architect pack, execution pack, capture pack, review pack, and Action Capsule.
Documentation must state exactly what Nilhan gives to whom and what must never be shared.
Weaker-agent documentation must use progressive disclosure and avoid unnecessary top-level cognitive load.
Plain-English explanations must accompany technical contracts.
Known limitations and human approval boundaries must be explicit.
Source-of-truth precedence must be documented.
Migration from v7 must be documented and must preserve v7 separately.
Stale version references and duplicate entry documents must be removed or clearly archived.
External guide inputs must be recorded by exact path, version, and SHA-256 rather than assumed.
The full source tree must pass JSON validation, Python compilation, tests, adversarial checks, and secret scanning before packaging.
The release candidate must be built from a clean checkout in a clean environment.
The completed ZIP must be safely extracted into another clean location and the complete suite rerun there.
The installable package must install and execute in a clean environment.
Every final file claimed in a completion message must physically exist and be reopened and verified.
Release evidence must be written before the human-facing completion statement.
A release must not be declared complete while any P0/P1 defect, test failure, missing sandbox validation, or missing real-agent evaluation remains.
Artifact durability and retrieval must be treated as a release gate, preventing a repeat of the lost v8 artifact incident.
No runtime database in any release artifact: only migrations and initialization code ship.
Stable security exception contract: every authorization denial uses one typed hierarchy and machine-readable code.

Authority Envelope: every capsule is bound to issuer, authenticated actor, role, project, broker/provider audience, action-definition digest, base seal, state version, policy, model profile, skills, capsule ID, attempt sequence, issue time, expiry, and revocation epoch.
Uncertainty Ledger: unknown facts cannot silently become assumptions or requirements.
Context provenance labels: agents can distinguish signed instructions, trusted truth, evidence, and untrusted content.
Capability revocation: connected privileged capsules can be invalidated within a measured propagation bound; offline v8.0 remains verification/read-only.
Policy/model/skill drift guard: changes invalidate incompatible outstanding capsules.
Non-interference analysis: parallel work is allowed only when independence is proven.
Critical-task quorum: high-impact work requires the configured genuinely independent verifier quorum and any required human approval; Architect adjudication cannot substitute for critical quorum.
Secret lifecycle controls: ephemeral injection, redaction, and non-persistence.
Resource and cost circuit breakers: prevent runaway attempts and denial-of-wallet.
Degradation without deception: unavailable components block affected actions rather than silently weakening assurance.
Replayable trace bundles: checks and decisions can be independently reconstructed where technically feasible.
Schema/version migration discipline: no guessing when trusted record formats evolve.
Profile drift monitoring: model behavior is recalibrated as providers and models change.
Evidence escrow: canonical proof is held outside the builder’s write authority while a signed project-facing index remains under the root `Evidence/` folder.
External release verification: signatures and attestations must be verifiable outside the build workspace.
Artifact durability gate: existence, stat, hash, extraction, and retest are performed after the artifact is moved to its final durable location.
Security Spine before agents: no real calibration, capture, or implementation against untrusted content before identity, policy, broker, sandbox, evidence, and emergency-stop mediation exists.
Alpha vertical proof: the smallest real base-to-result-to-review-to-promotion workflow is proven before horizontal platform expansion.
Architect output is untrusted: deterministic policy and protected human/quorum gates sit between every model and privileged authority.
Base/result promotion protocol: agents edit isolated disposable clones; accepted broker-owned deltas are cleanly reconstructed and promoted with Git compare-and-swap under authoritative control.
Separated action aggregates: definition, capsule, attempt, review, and criterion lifecycles no longer share one ambiguous state machine.
Typed cryptographic envelope: domain separation, exact bytes, trust bootstrap, key purpose, rotation, revocation, anti-downgrade, and compromise recovery replace “sign JSON” ambiguity.
Race-safe cross-platform paths: final-handle containment and Windows reparse/alias semantics are part of the supported-platform contract.
Indeterminate side effects: outbox, `OUTCOME_UNKNOWN`, reconciliation, and no automatic irreversible retry replace implied exactly-once behavior.
Tamper-evident language and external anchors: local chains no longer overclaim immutability.
Preregistered evaluation: primary estimands, fair baselines, partitions, power, uncertainty, stopping rules, and holdout contamination controls govern efficacy claims.
Machine-readable release gates: stable blocker IDs and per-environment support rows replace narrative-only release decisions.
Incident and trust recovery: kill switch, mass revocation, quarantine, key rotation, recovery points, revalidation, and re-attestation are release-tested.
Continuous operator and migration work: CLI, docs, migration, platform support, and performance budgets begin before Alpha rather than after evaluation.

scope_note: P0/P1 mapping is provisional until the post-Alpha scope freeze.
